meta.architecture_style: Multi-platform monorepo: iOS MVVM+RxSwift, Android MVVM, Firebase serverless backend
meta.executive_summary: AroundSound is a geotagged audio recording and sharing app for iOS and Android backed by Firebase. The iOS app uses MVVM with RxSwift reactive bindings, Swinject DI, and UIKit. The Android app uses MVVM with native Android patterns. The backend is Firebase Cloud Functions (TypeScript/Node.js) with Firestore, Cloud Storage, FCM, and BigQuery. Architecture separates Presentation, ViewModel, Repository, Provider, and Networking layers on iOS with protocol-based abstractions throughout.

file_placement_rules.description: All iOS ViewModels live here; cell-specific VMs in AlbumCellTypes/
All iOS screen controllers; animators in Animators/, popups in Popup/
Computed/filtered views of repository data for specific use cases
Bidirectional DTO-to-domain model transformations; one file per entity
Domain entities in root; DTOs in Dtos/; Notification models in Notification/
One file per domain entity; index.ts exports all functions

naming_conventions.description: All error types prefixed AS; domain-specific subtypes in own files
DTOs suffixed Dto; located in Models/Dtos/
Backend source files use snake_case; exported function names use camelCase

architectural_style.title: MVVM + RxSwift reactive architecture on iOS
architectural_style.chosen: MVVM with RxSwift Observables/Drivers, PublishRelay inputs, protocol-based DI via Swinject
architectural_style.rationale: Enables testable ViewModels via protocol mocks; reactive streams unify Firebase real-time updates, local changes, and UI events into single data pipelines

key_decisions.title: Firebase as sole backend
key_decisions.chosen: Firestore + Cloud Functions + Storage + FCM + Auth
key_decisions.rationale: Single vendor covers all backend needs: real-time sync, serverless compute, file storage, push notifications, auth

key_decisions.title: RxSwift for state management
key_decisions.chosen: RxSwift PublishRelay/BehaviorRelay with Driver for UI binding
key_decisions.rationale: Unidirectional data flow; Driver guarantees main-thread delivery; no accidental stream termination with Relay

key_decisions.title: Swinject for dependency injection
key_decisions.chosen: Centralized Swinject container initialized in AppDelegate
key_decisions.rationale: Explicit protocol-based registration enables mock substitution for testing; all dependencies resolved at init time

key_decisions.title: Protocol-based abstractions for all services
key_decisions.chosen: Every major service has a Protocol variant (FirebaseProviderProtocol, GeneralPlayerProtocol, etc.)
key_decisions.rationale: Enables unit testing via mock conformance without framework coupling

key_decisions.title: Serverless Firebase Cloud Functions for backend logic
key_decisions.chosen: TypeScript Cloud Functions triggered by HTTP and Firestore events
key_decisions.rationale: No server management; scales automatically; integrates natively with Firestore triggers for cascading operations

trade_offs.accept: Swinject container wires ~15 dependencies per ViewModel
trade_offs.benefit: Full testability and loose coupling across all layers

trade_offs.accept: RxSwift learning curve and DisposeBag management overhead
trade_offs.benefit: Unified reactive pipeline from Firebase listeners to UI with error propagation

trade_offs.accept: Python 2.7 admin tools with pinned exact versions
trade_offs.benefit: Simple maintenance scripts without infrastructure; runs with ApplicationDefault credentials

key_files.description: Creates typed errors with analytics logging
Routes errors to dialogs/toasts based on severity

patterns.name: PublishRelay Input / Driver Output
patterns.when_to_use: All ViewModel and Provider data flow on iOS
patterns.how_it_works: PublishRelay accepts user intent; flatMapLatest calls Firebase; result emitted as Driver to ViewController

patterns.name: RxSwift scan() state accumulation
patterns.when_to_use: When multiple input actions must accumulate into a state dictionary
patterns.how_it_works: scan() operator reduces InputType enum actions (.add/.merge/.reset) into immutable state copies

patterns.name: Firebase Firestore real-time listeners
patterns.when_to_use: Live album/recording sync between Firestore and iOS client
patterns.how_it_works: FirebaseProvider attaches snapshot listeners returning Observable; errors propagate to errorSubject

patterns.name: Firebase Cloud Functions HTTPS calls
patterns.when_to_use: Complex server-side operations requiring auth or cascading Firestore writes
patterns.how_it_works: iOS calls callable HTTPS functions via Firebase SDK; TypeScript handlers validate and execute

patterns.name: Observable.merge error aggregation
patterns.when_to_use: Surfacing errors from multiple async sources to single UI error handler
patterns.how_it_works: errorOutput merges PublishSubjects from all providers/managers into single Observable<ASError>
examples: MainViewModel.errorOutput merges location, share, sync, album provider errors

integrations.purpose: Email/Password + Google Sign-In; JWT token for Firestore rules
Push notifications for comments, likes, album events
Analytics event aggregation from Cloud Functions
OAuth2 identity returning Firebase Auth idToken

pattern_selection_guide.rationale: Relay never completes; safe for UI lifecycle

pattern_selection_guide.scenario: Live Firestore data needed in UI
pattern_selection_guide.rationale: Driver guarantees main thread; no nil emissions
Bypasses client security constraints; atomic server operations

pattern_selection.reactive_input: PublishRelay<T> for user actions; never use Subject for inputs
pattern_selection.reactive_output: Driver<T> for UI-bound outputs; Observable<T> for non-UI chains
pattern_selection.local_persistence: Disk library for file-based caching; SettingsManager for user preferences
pattern_selection.di_registration: Register in DepedencyContainer.swift; resolve via Resolver.swift

error_mapping.description: Wraps Firestore/Auth SDK errors; detects -1009 no-internet
Share link generation and access control failures

run_commands.ios_setup: cd iOS/AroundSound && pod install && open AroundSound.xcworkspace

frontend.rendering_strategy: Imperative UIKit with programmatic and XIB-based layout; RxDataSources for reactive table updates

ui_components.name: MainViewController
ui_components.description: Root container coordinating tab navigation, RecorderView, mini/fullscreen player

ui_components.name: AlbumDetailsViewController
ui_components.description: Album content with recordings, comments, editing, and sharing

ui_components.name: RecorderView
ui_components.description: Audio recording UI with waveform and location capture

ui_components.name: PlayerView / MiniPlayer / FullscreenPlayer
ui_components.description: Playback controls with progress scrubbing; mini and fullscreen variants

ui_components.name: TrimmingViewController
ui_components.description: Audio crop interface with waveform visualization and knob controls

ui_components.name: OnboardingViewController
ui_components.description: Multi-step auth and permissions onboarding flow

ui_components.name: PopupViewController
ui_components.description: Reusable modal with custom dismiss animation

state_management.approach: RxSwift unidirectional data flow: PublishRelay inputs → flatMapLatest → Driver outputs
state_management.server_state: Firebase Firestore real-time listeners via FirebaseProvider snapshot subscriptions
state_management.local_state: ViewModel-owned BehaviorSubject/PublishSubject; Disk library for persistent cache; SettingsManager for preferences
state_management.rationale: No global store; each ViewModel owns its slice; reactive merging via Observable.merge() for multi-source outputs

routing.description: Switches between OnboardingViewController and MainViewController based on auth state
Tab-based hub for albums, sounds, profile
Deeplink entry point for shared album previews

data_fetching.name: Firestore Real-time Listener
data_fetching.mechanism: FirebaseProvider snapshot listener wrapped in Observable; merged into repository Driver
data_fetching.when_to_use: Albums, recordings, comments needing live updates

data_fetching.name: Firebase Cloud Function Call
data_fetching.mechanism: Firebase Functions SDK HTTPS callable; returns Single/Observable
data_fetching.when_to_use: Server-side operations: sharing, link generation, member management

data_fetching.name: Disk Local Cache
data_fetching.mechanism: Disk.read() on app launch; merged with Firebase live stream
data_fetching.when_to_use: Offline-first display of recordings and album visibility state

frontend.styling: UIKit Auto Layout with SnapKit DSL; ColorUtil for centralized color constants; no SwiftUI
key_conventions: Use Driver (not Observable) for all ViewController bindings to guarantee main thread
Use PublishRelay (not Subject) for input ports to prevent accidental stream termination
Dispose all subscriptions via DisposeBag scoped to ViewController lifecycle
Always use ASErrorFactory to create errors; never instantiate error types directly
Converters must be pure functions; no side effects in DTO-to-domain transformation

developer_recipes.task: Add a new Firebase-backed feature (e.g. new entity CRUD)
steps: 1. Create Dto in Models/Dtos/ and domain model in Models/; write Converter in Networking/Converters/
2. Add Firestore methods to FirebaseProvider.swift (return Observable); update FirebaseProviderProtocol
3. Create Provider in Domain/Provider/ with PublishRelay inputs and Driver outputs; register in DepedencyContainer.swift
4. Inject Provider into ViewModel; expose Output Driver for ViewController binding
5. Add Cloud Function handler in platform/firebase/functions/src/<domain>.ts; export in index.ts; run npm run build
1. Create <Name>ViewController.swift in ViewControllers/; create <Name>ViewModel.swift in ViewModel/
2. Define ViewModel struct with injected dependencies and expose Driver outputs + PublishRelay inputs
3. Register ViewModel in DepedencyContainer.swift; add resolve helper in Resolver.swift
4. In ViewController.viewDidLoad() bind ViewModel outputs to UI via .drive(); bind UI events to ViewModel inputs via .bind(to:); add all to disposeBag

developer_recipes.task: Deploy Firebase Cloud Functions to dev
steps: 1. cd platform/firebase/functions && npm run lint && npm run build (compiles TS to lib/)
2. Trigger GitLab CI manual job 'deploy aroundsound-dev' OR run bash deploy-to-environment.sh locally

developer_recipes.task: Run a bulk data operation using admin tools
steps: 2. Set GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS to service account JSON for target project

architecture_diagram:
graph TD
    AppDelegate[AppDelegate / DI Init] --> RootVC[RootViewController]
    RootVC --> OnboardVC[OnboardingViewController]
    RootVC --> MainVC[MainViewController]
    MainVC --> AlbumsVC[AlbumsViewController]
    MainVC --> SoundsVC[SoundsViewController]
    MainVC --> AudioEngine[AudioEngine\nGeneralPlayer/Recorder]
    AlbumsVC --> AlbumDetailsVC[AlbumDetailsViewController]
    AlbumsVC --> AlbumsVM[AlbumsViewModel]
    AlbumsVM --> AlbumProvider[AlbumProvider\nDomain Provider]
    AlbumProvider --> FirebaseProv[FirebaseProvider\nNetworking]
    FirebaseProv --> Firestore[(Cloud Firestore)]
    FirebaseProv --> CloudFn[Firebase Cloud\nFunctions]
    CloudFn --> Firestore
    FirebaseProv --> Storage[(Cloud Storage\nAudio Files)]
    AudioEngine --> AVFoundation[AVFoundation]

pitfalls.description: Using PublishSubject as input relay will terminate the entire stream on onError(); downstream Providers stop emitting permanently
pitfalls.recommendation: Always use PublishRelay for input ports; reserve PublishSubject only for error subjects that feed errorOutput

pitfalls.description: Firestore real-time listeners are long-lived and not automatically removed when a ViewController is dismissed; causes memory leaks and phantom updates
pitfalls.recommendation: Store the Firestore unsubscribe function and call it in deinit or on navigation away; ensure DisposeBag is deallocated with ViewController

pitfalls.description: DepedencyContainer.swift registers services in a specific order; circular dependencies or resolving before registration causes silent nil crashes
pitfalls.recommendation: Always register leaf dependencies (ErrorFactory, Analytics) before composite services (Providers, ViewModels) in DepedencyContainer.swift

pitfalls.description: Binding an Observable (not Driver) to UIKit elements can crash if the observable emits on a background thread from Firestore callbacks
pitfalls.recommendation: Convert all ViewController-bound outputs to Driver via .asDriver(onErrorJustReturn:) before exposing from ViewModels

pitfalls.description: platform/tools/bin/ scripts use Python 2.7 syntax (urlparse, print statements, unicode literals); will fail silently on Python 3
pitfalls.recommendation: Always run admin tools with Python 2.7 interpreter; do not port to Python 3 without full regression on Firestore query outputs

implementation_guidelines.capability: Reactive Audio Recording with State Management
implementation_guidelines.pattern_description: GeneralRecorder wraps AVAudioRecorder in a protocol-based class emitting recordingStatus as Driver<RecordingStatus> and newlyCreatedRecording as Observable<RawLocalRecording>; state transitions via delegate callbacks update internal BehaviorSubject
tips: recordingStatus is Driver (main thread, no error); newlyCreatedRecording is Observable (handle threading explicitly)
Request permission via recordingPermission() before calling start(); check iOS 14.5+ privacy requirements
Recorder disables MPRemoteCommandCenter during recording to avoid control center interference

implementation_guidelines.capability: Reactive Audio Playback with Remote Transport Controls
implementation_guidelines.pattern_description: GeneralPlayer merges two underlying players (local AVAudioPlayer + remote AVPlayer) via Observable.merge(); exposes percentage Driver and playbackStatus Driver; integrates MPRemoteCommandCenter for Control Center controls
tips: currentRecordingInput is ReplaySubject(bufferSize:1); late subscribers receive last value immediately
Call stopCurrentRecording() before releasing player to avoid AVAudioPlayer memory leaks
seek(valueFromSlider:) takes normalized 0.0-1.0; seekTo(value:) takes absolute seconds

implementation_guidelines.capability: Firestore Real-time Sync with Reactive Repositories
implementation_guidelines.pattern_description: FirebaseProvider wraps Firestore CRUD in Observable.create{}; Repositories merge disk cache + Firebase live updates + runtime changes via Observable.merge(); errors propagate via .catchError(to: errorSubject)
tips: Use PublishRelay for inputs; never Subject (prevents accidental completion of album stream)
Converters (AlbumConverter, RecordingConverter) must be pure functions; no Firebase calls inside converters
Backend Cloud Functions must validate request.auth manually; firestore.rules enforces field-level access

implementation_guidelines.pattern_description: AppDelegate initializes DepedencyContainer at launch; all services registered as singletons or transients; ViewControllers resolve dependencies via Resolver; all injectable types have Protocol variants enabling mock substitution in tests
tips: Register leaf services first (ErrorFactory, Analytics) before composites (Providers, ViewModels)
All ViewModels are structs with constructor injection of ~10-15 dependencies
Never use property injection; all dependencies must be resolved at initialization time

implementation_guidelines.capability: Typed Error Hierarchy with Analytics Integration
implementation_guidelines.pattern_description: ASErrorFactory creates domain-typed errors (FirebaseError, RecordingError, ShareError, PersistenceError, GeneralError) with analytics logging; errors propagate via PublishSubject.onNext() to errorOutput observables merged in ViewModels; ASErrorRouter routes to UI
implementation_guidelines.usage_example: let error = errorFactory.createFirebaseError(kind: .readFailed, externalError: err); errorSubject.onNext(error)
tips: ASErrorFactory.createFirebaseError() detects NSError code -1009 for no-internet and provides specialized handling
Never instantiate FirebaseError/RecordingError directly; always use ASErrorFactory methods
errorOutput in ViewModels must merge all provider/manager error observables to avoid silent failures

development_rules.rule: Always use CocoaPods for iOS dependencies; never add Swift packages manually — Podfile is the sole source of truth for Firebase, RxSwift, Swinject, Nuke, SnapKit
Always run npm run lint then npm run build before deploying Firebase Functions — predeploy hooks enforce this; lib/index.js is the compiled artifact loaded at runtime
development_rules.source: platform/firebase/functions/package.json scripts: lint → tslint, build → tsc, main: lib/index.js

development_rules.rule: Never deploy to production (aroundsound-prod) from any branch other than master — GitLab CI enforces only: [master] on the prod deploy stage
development_rules.source: platform/.gitlab-ci.yml deploy aroundsound-prod stage only: [master] constraint

development_rules.rule: Always use environment-specific google-services.json for Android builds — debug builds use aroundsound-dev project; release builds use aroundsound-prod project
Always use PublishRelay (never PublishSubject) for ViewModel and Provider input ports — Relay prevents accidental stream termination via onError/onCompleted propagation
Always expose ViewController-bound outputs as Driver<T> (never Observable<T>) — Driver guarantees main-thread delivery and no-error contract required by UIKit bindings
Always run ./gradlew test before ./gradlew assembleDebug in Android CI — unit tests must pass before debug artifact is generated
development_rules.source: android/.gitlab-ci.yml debug_assemble stage runs gradlew test then gradlew assembleDebug sequentially

development_rules.rule: Always use npm for Firebase Functions dependencies in platform/firebase/functions/package.json — never edit package-lock.json manually; lock file is committed and must match CI environment
development_rules.source: platform/firebase/functions/package.json specifies firebase-admin, @google-cloud/bigquery with pinned versions

deployment.serverless_functions: Firebase Cloud Functions — platform/firebase/functions/src/*.ts compiled to lib/; deployed via firebase deploy --only functions
ci_cd: GitLab CI: platform/.gitlab-ci.yml — manual trigger for dev/prod Firebase deploy; master-only for prod
GitLab CI: android/.gitlab-ci.yml — ./gradlew test then ./gradlew assembleDebug on push
deployment.infrastructure_as_code: platform/firebase/firebase.json (Firebase project config); platform/firebase/firestore.rules (security); platform/firebase/storage.rules (storage ACL); platform/firebase/firestore.indexes.json (query indexes)
deployment.environment_config: platform/firebase/functions/src/config.ts for Cloud Functions env vars; android/app/src/{debug,release}/google-services.json per build variant; platform/firebase/.firebaserc sets default project (aroundsound-dev)
